I read through the PDF and it does not say the stats quoted. Furthermore, this research paper is based a 2014 survey, which is outdated. It is furthermore conducted by "Taiwan Alliance to End the Death Penalty (TAEDP)", which as its name suggests, is not neutral data. TAEDP is a strongly opinionated group that often gets criticized by the majority of Taiwanese for its controversial viewpoints.
/* Modern-day public opinion */ added opinion in Taiwan.

/* By country */ Map needs to be changed. It currently puts all countries with no executions in the past 10 years as orange/abolitionist in practice, whether or not they are actually classified as abolitionist in practice or retentionist.

/* Contemporary use */ The map has a powerful utility in that it visually displays the locations in the USA where there is capital punishment. However, the choice of the color red, which has connotations of violence and death, is totally inappropriate and not neutral. I hide the image until a more neutral image can be created or a replacement can be used.  See talk page for details.

/* Contemporary use */ It is a mistake to display this map when there is no source directly cited on this page or on the Wikimedia Commons page that justifies it. Once a map based on a source is created, or once this map is updated and or sourced, then and only then can the map appear on Wikipedia mainspace.
/* By country{{anchor|Global distribution}} */ The source cited explicitly says 1,000s and explicitly does not say 1,000+
